ref: But they did not sleep together before her baby was born. Then Joseph named him Jesus.
text: Amaa mò̱ aa mò̱ mo̱ŋ di ne̱ ɔɔ bo̱ ko̱we̱. Ne̱ ɔɔ ko̱we̱ ɔnyare̱, ne̱ Yo̱sɛf a sa kayaagyi‑o ke̱nyare̱ ɛ Yeesuu.
translated: The angel appeared to Joseph and said, "Joseph, son of David, do not be afraid to take Mary as your wife, for the child she has conceived is of the Holy Spirit. She will give birth to a son and you are to name him Jesus."

ref: When he heard that it was Jesus from Nazareth, he shouted, “Jesus, Son of David, have pity on me!”
text: Te̱napo̱ amo̱ a te kabo̱re̱ kɛɛ mfe̱ŋ ne̱ oo nu fe̱yɛ Yeesuu Nasarɛte̱nyi e̱ kyo̱ŋ kabo̱re̱‑ɔ se̱‑ɔ, ne̱ ɔɔ le̱e̱ ɔ fɛɛ‑rɔ keŋkeŋ fe̱yɛ, “Yeesuu, Deefid mò̱ nana, ŋu mo̱ e̱wɛɛ.”
translated: Then they cried out to him, “Jesus, Master, have mercy on us.”

ref: It is like what happens when a man goes away for a while and places his servants in charge of everything. He tells each of them what to do, and he orders the guard to keep alert.
text: A du ne̱e̱ fe̱yɛ kawurenyare̱ ko̱ a kpa a o tu kpa, ne̱ oo ke kusuŋ‑no sa mò̱ ayaafɔre̱ fe̱yɛ bo̱ waa, ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ lɔŋ ɔde̱e̱re̱se̱po̱ fe̱yɛ ɔ de̱e̱re̱ mò̱ lɔŋ se̱ bo̱ fo̱ saŋ ne̱ ɔ ba‑ɔ.
translated: If you want to be a good person, you must do good to others, and you will see that they will also do good to you, and everybody will be happy with you and love you, and you will love everybody and they will love you.

ref: When the Son of Man appears, things will be just as they were when Noah lived.
text: Ane̱ŋ ne̱ aa dɛɛ waa ane̱ ɔde̱daapo̱ Nowaa mbe̱e̱ se̱‑ɔ, ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ mo̱, dimaadi mò̱ gyi‑o, kikiŋŋi ba‑ɔ e̱ waa.
translated: I'm the one who brought the matter to Nowaa so that we will know, I'm the one who investigated, I searched thoroughly, I asked questions and brought it.

ref: When Jesus finished speaking, the crowds were surprised at his teaching.
text: Yeesuu a sa se̱ŋsa lo̱we̱‑ɔ, ne̱ e̱ye̱e̱ a kpe̱ŋ lamaŋ ne̱ baa nu mò̱ ase̱ŋ‑ɔ.
translated: Jesus had to go through Samaria on the way.

ref: Jesus replied, “I promise you before a rooster crows tonight, you will say three times that you don't know me.”
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu mɔ a be̱ŋŋaa mò̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ e̱ tɔwe̱ mo̱ i gywii fo̱ fe̱yɛ, fo̱ e̱ swe̱e̱ iluwi e̱sa kanye mɔ fe̱yɛ fo̱ mo̱ŋ nyi mo̱ pwɛɛ na kyaŋare̱ dɛɛ fo̱re̱.”
translated: “You Jesus, you are the one I have been talking about, saying, ‘There is one who will baptize with the Holy Spirit, and he will be greater than I, for he existed before I was born.’”

ref: You blind Pharisee! First clean the inside of a cup, and then the outside will also be clean.
text: Mo̱ne̱ Farisii awuye mɔ, mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ nyi fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ ya lee e̱bɔye̱ le̱e̱ mo̱ne̱ ŋkpo̱nɔ‑rɔ, amɔ mo̱ne̱ ŋyo̱wɔre̱ mo̱ŋ lɛɛ n de iyisi aaa?
translated: What will you do if you see your friend's mother, or your mother's co-wife, or your mother's sister, or your mother's brother's wife, and she is crying, what will you do?

ref: “Announce to the people of Jerusalem: ‘Your king is coming to you! He is humble and rides on a donkey. He comes on the colt of a donkey.’ ”
text: “Mo̱nꞌ tɔwe̱ gywii Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ awuye, fe̱yɛ, ‘Mo̱nꞌ ke̱e̱, mo̱ne̱ owure e̱ ba mo̱ne̱ ase̱‑ɔ. Ɔ gye̱ ɔwo̱rɔba, ne̱ ɔɔ de̱e̱ kakurumagyii ɔ ba.’ ”
translated: “The men who went up to Jerusalem said to him, ‘The man said to us, “You are not to enter, you are not to go around our town. He has a dog, and it will bite you if you do.”’”

ref: he said, “Get out of here! The little girl isn't dead. She is just asleep.” Everyone started laughing at Jesus.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ le̱e̱ mfe̱ŋ. Kakye̱e̱sɛɛ‑ɔ mo̱ŋ wu, o di idi ne̱e̱.” Mò̱ a tɔwe̱ ane̱ŋ‑ɔ, ne̱ baa mɔse̱ mò̱.
translated: The man called his wife and said, “My mother is coming. When she comes, you must cook, and then eat.” The woman called her mother-in-law, and they went to look for food.

ref: Herod was very sorry for what he had said. But he did not want to break the promise he had made in front of his guests.
text: A mo̱ŋ gyi owure‑o akatɔ pɛɛɛ, amaa ane̱ŋ ne̱ ɔɔ kyɔ waa e̱taŋ gye̱maŋ‑ɔ‑rɔ se̱‑ɔ, ɔ mo̱ŋ taare̱ kine.
translated: The elephant has a big ear, but it has a small hole, the elephant uses its ear to hear.

ref: Don't call anyone on earth your father. All of you have the same Father in heaven.
text: Ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ ma kaŋ te̱e̱ ɔko̱ swe̱e̱re̱ mɔ se̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ se̱, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ se̱ ko̱ŋko̱ wo̱re̱ kpeŋ ne̱ mo̱ne̱ de. Mò̱ e̱ gye̱ Wuribware̱.
translated: My mother gave birth to me at home, I was still small and she carried me on her back, she used to go to farm. My mother used to carry me on her back.

ref: Don't think I came to bring peace to the earth! I came to bring trouble, not peace.
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a kya se̱ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ nyi fe̱yɛ mo̱ a ba a m bo̱ baa kaye̱e̱yuri kaye̱ mɔ‑rɔ ne̱e̱ e̱e̱e̱? Daabii! Mo̱ a baa kaye̱ be̱be̱re̱ ne̱e̱.
translated: “Why is it that you are not able to do it, you who have such great faith? I tell you, if you had faith even as small as a mustard seed, you would be able to do it."

ref: But John kept objecting and said, “I ought to be baptized by you. Why have you come to me?”
text: Amaa Yohanee a le̱ŋ e̱ye̱e̱ fe̱yɛ o kyurowi Yeesuu mfɛɛre̱. Ɔ yɛ, “Weetee a bware fe̱yɛ fo̱ e̱ gye̱ ne̱ fo̱ bɔ mo̱ asuu, amaa fo̱ ya ko̱ŋ ba mo̱ ase̱ fe̱yɛ m bɔ fo̱ asuu.”
translated: The prophet John was in the desert, preaching and saying, “Prepare a path for the Lord, make his road straight. Baptize with water, but among you stands one whom you do not know, who is coming after you, but I am not worthy to untie his sandals.”

ref: Jesus said to the crowds and to his disciples:
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a sa se̱ŋsa gywii lamaŋ ne̱ ba nu mò̱ ase̱‑ɔ na mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ.
translated: Then Jesus said to them, "Let us go to the other side."

ref: He sent other servants to say to the guests, “The banquet is ready! My cattle and prize calves have all been prepared. Everything is ready. Come to the banquet!”
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ be̱e̱ suŋ ayaafɔre̱ ko̱ bamo̱ ase̱ bo̱ tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Mo̱ a waa ko̱ko̱fɔ‑rɔ a ateese‑o lo̱we̱. Mo̱ a mɔɔ mo̱ e̱naate̱‑rɔ e̱dabe̱dabe̱‑ɔ, ne̱ mo̱ a waa ke̱tɔ ke̱maa siraa lo̱we̱. Amo̱se̱‑ɔ mo̱nꞌ bo̱ gyi.’
translated: ‘You people of Bamo, you are very much loved, but you have turned your backs on us. You have really disappointed us, and we are very unhappy with you. What is wrong with you?’

ref: Jesus answered: Watch out and don't let anyone fool you!
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱nꞌ sa se̱ na ɔko̱ ma pe̱nna mo̱ne̱.
translated: Then Jesus said to his disciples, “You received free, now give free.”

ref: Jesus warned the disciples not to tell anyone about him. Jesus Speaks about His Suffering and Death (Matthew
text: Ne̱ Yeesuu a yii bamo̱ se̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ ma kaŋ tɔwe̱ amo̱‑ɔ gywii ɔke̱maa.” Ane̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ mò̱ ase̱ŋ kuŋu na mò̱ lowi ase̱ŋ‑ɔ (Matiyo
translated: Then Jesus said to them again, “Peace be to you; as the Father sent me, I also send you.” And having said this, he breathed on them, and said to them, “Receive the Holy Spirit.”

ref: You could have at least put my money in the bank, so I could have earned interest on it.”
text: Ne̱ e̱me̱ne̱ ya waa se̱ ne̱ fo̱ mo̱ŋ taa mo̱ atanne̱‑ɔ yaa bo̱ be̱ya baŋke̱‑rɔ, na mo̱ a kiŋŋi ba‑ɔ na ŋ nya atanne̱ be̱e̱ bo̱ dɔŋŋɔ se̱?’
translated: What will you do if you see your mother's husband's mother insulting you, and you are not yet married, and you are still a girl?

ref: If you love your father or mother or even your sons and daughters more than me, you are not fit to be my disciples.
text: Ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ kpa mò̱ se̱ bɛɛɛ mò̱ nyi ase̱ŋ a ɔ kyo̱ŋ mo̱‑ɔ mo̱ŋ fo̱ fe̱yɛ o kii mo̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ ɔko̱. Ɔke̱maa ne̱ ɔ be̱e̱ ɔ kpa mò̱ gyi‑ana ase̱ŋ a ɔ kyo̱ŋ mo̱‑ɔ, ane̱ŋ dɛɛ ne̱e̱.
translated: My mother said that she will give birth to a child and when the child is born she will bring it to her mother, then her mother will hold the child and care for it. My mother said that she will give birth to a child and when the child is born she will bring it.

ref: His son told him he would not do it, but later he changed his mind and went.
text: Ne̱ ɔɔ tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ se̱ fe̱yɛ, ‘Ma yɔ.’ Amaa ke̱tɔ a waa kafwe̱e̱‑ɔ, ne̱ oo nu mò̱ e̱ye̱e̱, ne̱ ɔɔ yɔ mò̱ se̱ a ko̱dɔɔ‑ɔ‑rɔ ya dɔɔ.
translated: He said to the owner of the farm, ‘My friend.’ But the owner of the farm was not happy with him, he gave him a kola nut, he said to him ‘My friend you will eat this and then you will be satisfied.’

ref: It will be an awful time for women who are expecting babies or nursing young children.
text: Ŋke nsi amo̱ adaŋpo̱kye̱e̱ na atrapo̱ i ŋu ase̱ŋ ne̱e̱ fɛɛ.
translated: The dog is running with the child on the farm.

ref: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet had said,
text: Amo̱‑ɔ a ba ne̱e̱ a ke̱tɔ ne̱ Wuribware̱ kyaamɛɛ ko̱ a kyo̱rɛɛ waa abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ‑ɔ nya waa kase̱ŋtiŋ fe̱yɛ,
translated: The man and his wife were there at Wuribware village when he heard that their abware child was sick

ref: Jesus and his disciples returned to Jerusalem. And as he was walking through the temple, the chief priests, the nation's leaders, and the teachers of the Law of Moses came over to him.
text: Ne̱ baa be̱e̱ kiŋŋi ya lwee Yɛro̱salɛm maŋ‑nɔ. Yeesuu a yɔ ɔ naa Wuribware̱ suŋkpa a kabuno‑o, ne̱ Wuribware̱ alɔŋŋɔpo̱ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ na Wuribware̱ mbraa akaapo̱po̱‑ɔ na maŋ‑ɔ abre̱sɛ‑ɔ a yɔ mò̱ ase̱.
translated: Then they will begin to say to the mountain, 'Fall on us,' and to the hills, 'Cover us.' For if they do these things when the wood is green, what will happen when it is dry?

ref: Then Jesus said to his disciples: If any of you want to be my followers, you must forget about yourself. You must take up your cross and follow me.
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a tɔwe̱ gywii mò̱ agyase̱po̱‑ɔ fe̱yɛ, “Ɔko̱ ne̱ ɔ kpa a ɔ gya mo̱ se̱‑ɔ yɔwe̱ ko̱waa ke̱tɔ ne̱ ke̱ e̱ pre̱ mò̱‑ɔ, na ɔ so̱rɔ mò̱ fɔŋfɔŋ kiyii kpare̱-abɛɛ‑rɔ gya mo̱ se̱.
translated: “He said to them, “If you are willing, I can do this for you; be clean.” And immediately the leprosy left them.

ref: Jesus answered: You are completely wrong! You don't know what the Scriptures teach. And you don't know anything about the power of God.
text: Mfe̱ŋ ne̱ Yeesuu a be̱ŋŋaa bamo̱ fe̱yɛ, “Mo̱ne̱ a fo̱ kpa, a le̱e̱ fe̱yɛ mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ nyi abware̱se̱ŋ wo̱re̱‑ɔ‑rɔ ase̱ŋ, ne̱ mo̱ne̱ mo̱ŋ nyi ane̱ŋ ne̱ Wuribware̱ ke̱yaale̱ŋ du‑o, ane̱ŋ dɛɛ mo̱ne̱ maa nu ke̱mo̱ kaase̱.
translated: “The Lord Jesus said to them, “Go and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit, and teaching them to do all that I have commanded you.”
